items.precondition: A complete requirement directory exists and includes all write-plan required files.
items.action: Run the requirements doctor command against that directory.
items.expected_result: The command exits 0 and prints a success summary or no error diagnostics.
items.evidence: Pytest case using a temporary valid requirement directory.

items.precondition: A requirement directory is missing at least one required write-plan input file.
items.action: Run the requirements doctor command against that directory.
items.expected_result: The command exits non-zero and reports VRD002 with the missing file path.
items.evidence: Pytest case asserting exit code and diagnostic output.

items.precondition: A requirement directory contains invalid JSON in contract.json or acceptance.json.
items.action: Run the requirements doctor command against that directory.
items.expected_result: The command exits non-zero and reports VRD003 for the invalid JSON file.
items.evidence: Pytest case asserting deterministic JSON parse diagnostics.

items.precondition: acceptance.json contains an item whose source does not reference a known goal or rule id from contract.json.
items.action: Run the requirements doctor command against that directory.
items.expected_result: The command exits non-zero and reports VRD006 for the acceptance item.
items.evidence: Pytest case asserting acceptance coverage diagnostics.

items.expected_result: All existing tests plus the new requirements doctor tests pass.

items.precondition: A requirement directory contains valid source files.
items.expected_result: No requirement source file is created, edited, or deleted by the command.
items.evidence: Code review plus automated test or temporary-directory content comparison.
